incorrects.description: Student selected 3 × 5: reversed the groups and items per group

params.description: All 5 circles highlight to show the group count.

steps.text: Count the circles first. That's the first number. How many circles do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 + 3: confused multiplication with addition

params.description: One circle highlights with its 3 dots visible.

steps.text: We use × when we have equal groups. The plus sign is for adding. Count the groups, then what's in each.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 + 5: confused multiplication with addition and reversed the values

steps.text: When you see equal groups, you multiply. The × shows groups of the same size. Which number is the groups?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts circles one by one (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), then opens one circle to show 3 dots (1, 2, 3).

steps.text: Let me show you. First, I count the circles: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Five circles. Those are my groups. Now I look inside one: 1, 2, 3. Three dots in each group. So I have 5 groups of 3. That's 5 × 3.
